Title: Comparative Transcriptomic and Lipidomic Analysis of Fatty Acid Accumulation in Three Camellia oleifera Varieties During Seed Maturing
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Oleosin family seed oil body structural protein
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ole transcripts were significantly upregulated in the high-oil variety HH2, especially at T3-T4. 2. This upregulation coincided with increased TAG biosynthetic gene expression and higher seed oil content. 3. The study suggests oleosin contributes to oil body stability and supports lipid accumulation during late seed maturation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy storage in seeds; edible oil quality and nutritional unsaturated fatty acid reservoir
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes storage and accumulation of seed oil/TAG in the high-oil variety
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to improve oil body stability and enhance oil accumulation, potentially combined with TAG assembly genes in breeding or engineering
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stages T1-T4 under field growth conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in late seed maturation, especially T3-T4 in HH2
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in kernels during late maturation in HH2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses Ole I-V collectively; no single oleosin isoform was functionally isolated as the sole causal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy storage and edible oil nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with increased carbon flux into fatty acid synthesis and higher oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/committed-step enzyme; possible feedback regulation discussed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; WGCNA; RT-qPCR; lipid profiling correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACCase was coordinately upregulated in the high-oil variety HH2 during T3-T4. 2. Its elevated expression coincided with increased oil accumulation. 3. The paper proposes low C18:1 may relieve feedback and favor ACCase-mediated entry into fatty acid synthesis, though this feedback remains speculative.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance precursor supply for oil biosynthesis, especially for late seed filling when combined with downstream TAG assembly genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in late maturation (T3-T4) in HH2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Kernel expression elevated in HH2 during late maturation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Figure 7 validates accA/accB-related expression trends by RT-qPCR.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation is associated with active fatty acid synthesis and high oil content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAS I showed coordinated high expression in HH2 during late maturation. 2. This pattern is part of a broader activation of the de novo fatty acid synthesis module linked to high oil content.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression correlates with more active fatty acid synthesis in high-oil seeds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAS II was among the fatty acid synthesis genes coordinately upregulated in HH2 at T3-T4. 2. Its expression is linked to elevated oil accumulation during late maturation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation is associated with enhanced de novo fatty acid synthesis and higher oil content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAS III expression was elevated in the high-oil variety HH2, especially late in maturation. 2. RT-qPCR supported RNA-seq trends. 3. Its induction accompanies increased oil accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression accompanies active fatty acid synthesis and increased oil content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KAR was significantly upregulated in HH2 during late maturation. 2. RT-qPCR confirmed RNA-seq trends. 3. Its expression is part of the coordinated de novo fatty acid synthesis program associated with high oil content.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation is linked to higher fatty acid biosynthetic activity and oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HAD was part of the late-stage coordinated upregulation in HH2. 2. This expression pattern is associated with enhanced oil accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression accompanies active de novo fatty acid synthesis and higher oil content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EAR was coordinately upregulated in HH2 during T3-T4. 2. Its expression pattern supports increased fatty acid synthetic flux in high-oil seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces the first double bond into stearoyl-ACP to produce oleoyl-ACP
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Oleic acid; downstream linoleic and linolenic acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional unsaturated fatty acids in edible oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes C18:1 formation; when paired with low downstream FAD expression, favors oleic acid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-control step for desaturation state
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; WGCNA; expression-metabolite comparison across varieties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SAD expression varied among varieties and was highlighted as a key determinant of fatty acid composition. 2. In the L variety, SAD was upregulated while FAD3/FAD7/FAD8 remained low, favoring C18:1 accumulation. 3. In HH2, SAD was also upregulated late, but stronger downstream desaturase expression shifted flux toward more polyunsaturated fatty acids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be targeted to modulate oleic acid levels, especially in combination with downstream FAD genes to tune oil quality
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in HH2 at T4; also stage-specific upregulation in L
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper interprets varietal fatty-acid profiles primarily through coordinated SAD and FAD expression differences.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Omega-3 fatty acid desaturase; ER membrane desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a third double bond into 18:2 linked to phosphatidylcholine to generate 18:3
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional polyunsaturated fatty acid in edible oil
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed kernels; TAG/bound fatty acid pool
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression increases C18:3 proportion and reduces relative C18:1 proportion
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for C18:3 accumulation in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; pathway expression analysis; metabolite profiling across varieties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD3 was upregulated in HH2 at T1-T2 and T3-T4, in XL108 at T2-T3, and showed little change in XL192. 2. Elevated FAD3 expression was associated with higher C18:3 proportion in the high-oil variety. 3. The study concludes that strong FAD3 expression contributes to increased linolenic acid accumulation during seed maturation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for increasing C18:3 in tea oil; should be balanced against desired oleic acid levels
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High and stage-specific upregulation in HH2; moderate in XL108; low/stable in XL192
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing C18:3 may lower oleic acid proportion and alter oil oxidative stability
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper specifically highlights FAD3 as a major contributor to varietal differences in C18:3.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Omega-6 fatty acid desaturase; plastid membrane desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a second double bond into 18:1 in plastid membrane lipids to produce 18:2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Linoleic acid; precursor to linolenic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression supports increased desaturation from C18:1 to C18:2 and subsequent C18:3 synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD6 was significantly upregulated during T2-T3 in HH2. 2. It was located in coexpression modules correlated with C18:2/C18:3. 3. The gene likely supports increased unsaturated fatty acid accumulation in high-oil seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FAD6 expression was validated in RT-qPCR figure.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Omega-3 fatty acid desaturase; plastid membrane desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a third double bond into 18:2 in plastid lipids to form 18:3
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional polyunsaturated fatty acid in edible oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression markedly increases C18:3 proportion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; WGCNA; metabolite profiling across varieties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Three FAD7 transcripts in HH2 were mainly upregulated at T1-T2 and T2-T3. 2. In XL108, FAD7 was mainly upregulated at T2-T3, while in XL192 only slight upregulation occurred. 3. High FAD7 expression was concluded to increase C18:3 content in HH2 seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering higher linolenic acid content, but oil quality goals must consider possible reduction in oleic acid proportion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong early-to-mid maturation upregulation in HH2; weaker in other varieties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce C18:1 proportion and potentially affect oxidative stability of the oil
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper repeatedly emphasizes FAD7 as one of the principal genes explaining varietal C18:3 differences.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Omega-3 fatty acid desaturase; plastid membrane desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a third double bond into 18:2 in plastid lipids to generate 18:3
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nutritional polyunsaturated fatty acid in edible oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression increases C18:3 proportion in high-oil seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; WGCNA; metabolite profiling across varieties
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAD8 appeared in the dark-green WGCNA module associated with C18:3. 2. It was upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2, nearly unchanged across stages in XL108, and only slightly upregulated in XL192. 3. The study concludes that high FAD8 expression contributes to elevated C18:3 content in HH2 seeds.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for boosting C18:3 during late seed maturation, potentially stacked with FAD3/FAD7
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong late-stage upregulation in HH2; weak or unchanged in other varieties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce oleic acid proportion and possibly lower oil oxidative stability
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FAD8 was specifically highlighted as present only in the dark-green module among the key C18:3-linked modules.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation supports active TAG assembly and higher oil content in HH2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; pathway expression analysis; lipid profiling correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPAAT was significantly upregulated in HH2 during T3-T4. 2. Its induction coincided with elevated TAG assembly and higher oil accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression supports TAG assembly and increased oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LACS was among genes highly expressed in HH2 during late seed maturation. 2. Its upregulation supports active acyl-CoA provision for TAG synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed energy storage; edible oil yield
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulation promotes TAG assembly and contributes to higher seed oil content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; WGCNA; pathway expression analysis; lipid profiling correlation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGAT transcripts in HH2 were upregulated at all developmental stages relative to the lower-oil varieties. 2. DGAT showed significant upregulation during T3-T4 together with LPAAT, LACS, and PDAT. 3. The data indicate that active TAG assembly is an important contributor to the high oil content of HH2.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime engineering target to increase seed oil yield, ideally stacked with precursor-supply and desaturation genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in HH2 across stages; especially strong at T3-T4
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to DGAT generally, without isolating a specific isoform.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression promotes TAG accumulation in high-oil seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; WGCNA; pathway expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PDAT was significantly upregulated in HH2 during T3-T4. 2. RT-qPCR supported the transcriptomic trend. 3. Increased PDAT expression likely contributes to high TAG accumulation in the high-oil variety.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to improve TAG assembly, particularly together with DGAT and upstream fatty acid synthesis genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The discussion cites recent evidence that WRI1 may regulate PDAT.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: glycerol backbone supply for TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Generates glycerol-3-phosphate, the glycerol backbone precursor for TAG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High expression provides sufficient glycerol backbone for TAG biosynthesis and supports oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. G3PDH showed high expression at T3-T4 in HH2. 2. The study interprets this as providing raw material for TAG biosynthesis. 3. Its induction is consistent with active TAG accumulation in the high-oil variety.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High at T3-T4 in HH2 kernels
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as G3PDH/G3PHD in text; interpreted here as glycerol-3-phosphate dehydrogenase.

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRI1 was significantly upregulated in HH2 during T3-T4. 2. Its induction coincided with coordinated activation of multiple de novo fatty acid synthesis genes and TAG assembly genes. 3. The study supports WRI1 as a central positive regulator of oil biosynthesis and suggests it may also regulate TAG-related genes such as G3PDH, DGAT, and PDAT.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: de novo fatty acid synthesis; TAG biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ACCase; EAR; HAD; KAR; KASI; KASII; KASIII; SAD; G3PDH; DGAT; PDAT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates multiple fatty acid synthesis and possibly TAG assembly genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator associated with high oil accumulation in the high-oil variety
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Seed storage oil and edible nutritional lipids
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes oil accumulation through coordinated activation of lipid biosynthetic pathways
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top candidate regulator for improving oil content; likely best deployed with downstream assembly genes to maximize yield
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly upregulated in HH2 at T3-T4
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Potential regulatory cooperation with LEC1-based seed maturation network
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed maturation/developmental cues; possibly phytohormone-linked networks inferred from WGCNA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior literature for WRI1 function and uses expression correlation rather than direct promoter-binding assays in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LEC1 was significantly upregulated in HH2 during T3-T4. 2. Its elevated expression coincided with strong activation of fatty acid synthesis genes and high oil accumulation. 3. The study interprets LEC1 as an important positive regulator of lipid biosynthesis, likely acting through WRI1-associated seed development networks.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: WRI1; fatty acid synthesis pathway genes (indirectly inferred)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively associated with activation of WRI1 and downstream fatty acid synthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important developmental regulator promoting high oil content during late maturation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes high oil accumulation during late seed maturation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful developmental regulator for increasing oil accumulation, especially in seed-specific engineering strategies
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed maturation stage comparison among varieties
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly upregulated in HH2 at T3-T4
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Seed developmental transcriptional network including ABI3/WRI1 inferred from prior literature cited by paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence in this paper is correlative/coexpression-based rather than direct functional perturbation.
